1. **John "J.D." Dorian**: Played by Zach Braff, J.D. is the main character and narrator of the series. He starts as a medical intern at Sacred Heart Hospital.

3. **Christopher Turk**: Played by Donald Faison, Turk is J.D.'s best friend and a surgical intern. Their friendship is a central element of the show.

4. **Carla Espinosa**: Portrayed by Judy Reyes, Carla is a nurse at Sacred Heart Hospital who eventually becomes Turk's wife.

5. **Dr. Perry Cox**: Played by John C. McGinley, Dr. Cox is a senior attending physician and J.D.'s mentor, known for his sarcastic and tough-love approach.

6. **Dr. Bob Kelso**: Portrayed by Ken Jenkins, Dr. Kelso is the Chief of Medicine at Sacred Heart Hospital, often seen as the antagonist due to his bureaucratic and profit-driven mindset.

7. **The Janitor**: Played by Neil Flynn, the Janitor is a mysterious and often antagonistic figure who frequently interacts with J.D., usually in a humorous or adversarial manner.

These characters form the core group around which the series revolves, and their interactions and development are central to the show's narrative.

question_text: What are the different geological fracture zones ?

['Chile']: The Chile Fracture Zone (CFZ) is a major  strike slip fault  and  fracture zone  in the  Chile Rise . The Chile Fracture Zone runs in an eastwest direction almost parallel to nearby  Juan Fernandez Ridge  and makes up a large part of the  Antarctic -- Nazca Plate  boundary.
['Mocha']: Mocha Fracture Zone (MFZ) is a  fracture zone  on the  Nazca Plate  off the coast of  Mocha Island ,  Chile . Mocha Fracture Zone trends from the  Valdivia Fracture Zone  in the south to the  Peru-Chile Trench  axis 100 km west of Mocha Island.
['Valdivia']: The Valdivia Fracture Zone (VFZ) is a  transform fault  zone off the coast of  southern Chile  which runs between the continental slope near  Valdivia  in Chile and the  Panov Seamount  crossing the  East Pacific Rise .
['Easter']: The Easter Fracture Zone is an oceanic  fracture zone  associated with the  transform fault  extending from the  Tuamotu archipelago  in the west to the  Peru-Chile Trench  to the east. The Easter Fracture Zone extends roughly 5900 kilometers from 20degS,131degW to 26degS,78degW. The landscape consists of several ridges and isolated  volcano s with maximal peak elevation above the seafloor of 3000m. Because the local seafloor has depths around 4000m to the north of the fracture zone and 3400m to the south of the fracture zone, most of its volcanic peaks form  seamount s. They do rise above sea level at the  Pitcairn Islands  and  Easter Island .
['Vema ']: This narrow ridge must have risen as a single vertical slab 6.6 million years ago. Up to three million years ago the ridge was at or even above  sealevel . Later it  subsided  to its present depth of 450 meters. Lema Fracture Zone. While the transform valley of the Vema Fracture Zone has a V-shaped profile, the next fracture zone to the south has a steep vertical wall on its southern side rising from a horizontal valley bottom. As an L would fit in well here, this fracture zone is nicknamed Lema Fracture Zone. The north-south distance between the Vema and Lema fracture zones is 70 km.

Ridge jump. Up to 2.2 million years ago the Vema transform fault was 40 km shorter than today, and the Lema fracture zone had a leftward offset of 40 km. Then the old spreading valley between Lema and Vema died, and spreading started from an axis 40 km to the east. The Vema transform was thus extended by 40 km, while the Lema fracture zone was reduced to zero offset. Spreading from the new axis has meanwhile increased the distance between the old and the new axis to 80 km.
['Kane ']: The Kane Fracture Zone is a medium-offset  transform fault zone  located on the  Mid-Atlantic Ridge  (MAR). It lies just to the north of the Kane Megamullion, an  oceanic core complex  that forms the  footwall  of a long-lived low-angle detachment fault. The Mid-Atlantic Ridge near the Kane Fracture zone (known as the MARK area) has been the focus of detailed scientific investigations, notably Expeditions 106 and 109 of the  Ocean Drilling Program , as well as numerous other geological and geophysical studies.
['Charlie-Gibbs Name from GEBCO gazettee:
The fault was named Charlie Fracture Zone after the  USCG  Ocean Weather Station Charlie at , athwart the fault. In July 1968 conducted a more extended survey. It was proposed that the fracture zone be renamed Gibbs Fracture Zone, as fracture zones are generally named for research vessels. The proposal was accepted only in part, and currently the official name is Charlie-Gibbs Fracture Zone. Note that the double name refers to the two parallel fracture zones together. The individual fracture zones have to be referred to as Charlie-Gibbs North and South. Seamounts. The transform area contains two named seamounts:
Protected area. The Charlie-Gibbs Marine Protected Area is a conservation area in the Charlie-Gibbs fracture zone in North Atlantic international waters.

Charlie-Gibbs Fracture Zone is a system of two parallel  fracture zone s. It is the most prominent interruption of the  Mid-Atlantic Ridge  between the  Azores  and  Iceland . It can be traced over more than 2000 kilometers, all the way from north-east of  Newfoundland  to south-west of  Ireland . It took 90 million years for the fault to grow to this length. Structure. The  transform fault  of the southern fracture zone displaces the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, coming from the  Azores Triple Junction , to the west over a distance of 120 km. At  longitude  31.75W a south to north  seismically active   rift valley  with a length of 40 km connects the western end of the southern transform to the eastern end of the northern transform.

Here is a comprehensive list of geological fracture zones:

1. **Clarion Fracture Zone**: Located in the Pacific Ocean, this fracture zone is part of the Clarion-Clipperton Zone, known for its rich deposits of polymetallic nodules. It was discovered by the Scripps Institution of Oceanography in 1954.

2. **Clipperton Fracture Zone**: Also part of the Clarion-Clipperton Zone, this fracture zone lies in the Pacific Ocean and is significant for its potential in deep-sea mining due to the abundance of manganese, nickel, copper, and cobalt.

3. **Romanche Fracture Zone**: Situated in the Atlantic Ocean, this fracture zone is one of the largest and most significant in the equatorial Atlantic, playing a crucial role in the movement of oceanic plates.

4. **Charlie-Gibbs Fracture Zone**: Found in the North Atlantic Ocean, this fracture zone is a major transform fault that offsets the Mid-Atlantic Ridge, influencing oceanic circulation patterns.

5. **Mendocino Fracture Zone**: Located off the coast of Northern California, this fracture zone is a transform fault that marks the boundary between the Pacific Plate and the Gorda Plate.

6. **Molokai Fracture Zone**: This fracture zone is located in the Pacific Ocean and is part of a series of fracture zones that extend from the Hawaiian Islands.

7. **Sovanco Fracture Zone**: Found in the northeastern Pacific Ocean, this fracture zone connects the Juan de Fuca Ridge with the Explorer Ridge, playing a role in the tectonic activity of the region.

8. **Blanco Fracture Zone**: Situated off the coast of Oregon, this fracture zone is a transform fault that separates the Juan de Fuca Plate from the Pacific Plate.

9. **Atlantis Fracture Zone**: Located in the Atlantic Ocean, this fracture zone is part of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge system and is known for its hydrothermal vent activity.

10. **Vema Fracture Zone**: This fracture zone is in the South Atlantic Ocean and is notable for its role in the offset of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge.

11. **Siqueiros Fracture Zone**: Found in the Pacific Ocean, this fracture zone is part of the East Pacific Rise and is characterized by its seismic activity.

12. **Quebrada Fracture Zone**: Located in the Pacific Ocean, this fracture zone is part of the complex tectonic setting of the East Pacific Rise.

13. **Discovery Fracture Zone**: Situated in the South Atlantic Ocean, this fracture zone is part of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge system and contributes to the region's tectonic dynamics.

14. **Heezen Fracture Zone**: Found in the North Atlantic Ocean, this fracture zone is part of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge and plays a role in the movement of oceanic plates.

15. **Tharp Fracture Zone**: Located in the South Atlantic Ocean, this fracture zone is named after the pioneering geologist Marie Tharp and is part of the Mid-Atlantic Ridge system.

These fracture zones are significant geological features that influence tectonic activity, oceanic circulation, and the distribution of marine resources.

   A highly acclaimed platformer where Mario and Luigi embark on a journey to save the Mushroom World from Bowser and his Koopalings, introducing new power-ups like the Super Leaf.

4. **Mario Bros. (1983)**  
   This arcade-style game was ported to the Famicom, featuring Mario and Luigi as plumbers who must defeat creatures emerging from the sewers.

5. **Dr. Mario (1990)**  
   A puzzle game where Mario takes on the role of a doctor, using capsules to eliminate viruses in a Tetris-like gameplay format.

6. **Donkey Kong (1983)**  
   Originally an arcade game, this was one of Mario's earliest appearances, where he must rescue Pauline from the titular ape.

7. **Donkey Kong Jr. (1983)**

    Mario serves as the referee in this sports game, further expanding his roles in various game genres.

    Mario appears as the referee in this boxing game, adding to his diverse roles across different game types.

These games highlight Mario's extensive presence on the Family Computer, showcasing his versatility across various genres beyond his traditional platforming roots.

question_text: Rhaetian Railway maintains which entity?

['Castielertobel Viaduct']:
the castielertobel viaduct () is a single track railway bridge spanning the castielertobelbach, and linking the municipalities of castiel and calfreisen, in the canton of graubunden, switzerland. it was built between 1913 and 1914 for the chur-arosa railway, and is now owned and used by the rhaetian railway.
location.
the viaduct is located on the rhaetian railway's metre gauge line from chur to the holiday and recreation resort of arosa (the chur-arosa line), and links calfreisen with castiel, just to the west of the luen-castiel railway station.
after the langwieser viaduct and the grundjitobel viaduct, the castielertobel viaduct is the third largest bridge on the arosa line.
['Schmittentobel Viaduct']:
the schmittentobel viaduct () is a single track limestone railway viaduct. it is situated near schmitten, in the canton of graubunden, switzerland.
the viaduct was opened in 1903 by the rhaetian railway, which still owns and uses it today.
an important element of the world heritage-listed albula railway, the viaduct is high, long, and has seven spans, each in length.
location.
the schmittentobel viaduct forms part of the albula railway section between tiefencastel and filisur.
within sight of the schmittentobel viaduct, a little further along the line towards filisur, is the much better known landwasser viaduct, which is one of the signature structures of the albula railway, and indeed the whole rhaetian railway.
technical data.
the schmittentobel viaduct is high, long, and has no main span.
['Langwieser Viaduct']:
the langwieser viaduct (or langwies viaduct; ) is a single track reinforced concrete railway bridge spanning the plessur river and the sapunerbach, near langwies, in the canton of graubunden, switzerland.
it was designed by and constructed between 1912 and 1914 by for the chur-arosa railway. at the time of its completion, the langwieser viaduct was the first railway bridge anywhere in the world to possess a span of 100 meters, as well as the first railway bridge of such a scale to be made of concrete.
presently, the langwieser viaduct is owned and used by the rhaetian railway; it remains the biggest bridge on the company's network.
['Solis Viaduct']:
the solis viaduct () is a single track eleven-arched limestone railway viaduct. it spans the albula east of the hamlet of solis, in the canton of graubunden, switzerland.
designed by the engineer hans studer, it was built in 1902 by munari, cayre und marasi for the rhaetian railway, which still owns and uses it today.
['Wiesen Viaduct']:
the wiesen viaduct (or wiesener viaduct; ) is a single-track railway viaduct, made from concrete blocks with dimension stone coverage. it spans the landwasser southwest of the hamlet of wiesen, in the canton of grisons, switzerland.
designed by the then chief engineer of the rhaetian railway, henning friedrich, it was built between 1906 and 1909 by the contractor g.
['Landwasser Viaduct']:
the landwasser viaduct () is a single-track six-arched curved limestone railway viaduct. it spans the landwasser between schmitten and filisur, in the canton of graubunden, switzerland.
designed by alexander acatos, the landwasser viaduct was constructed between 1901 and 1902 by muller & zeerleder on behalf of the rhaetian railway, which both owns and operates it through to the present day.
['Brusio spiral viaduct']:
it was first opened on 1 july 1908.
a key structure of the world heritage-listed bernina railway, it is located near brusio, in the canton of graubunden, switzerland, and was built to limit the railway's gradient at that location within its specified maximum of 7%. it is considered to be one of the architectural highlights of the rhaetian railway.
location.
the brusio spiral viaduct forms part of the bernina railway section between brusio and campascio.
['Grundjitobel Viaduct']:
the grundjitobel viaduct (or grundjitobel bridge; ) is a single track reinforced concrete railway bridge, spanning the grundjitobelbach near langwies, in the canton of graubunden, switzerland.
designed by hermann schurch, it was built between late june 1913 and early july 1914 by muller, zeerleder und gobat, for the chur-arosa railway. it is now owned and used by the rhaetian railway.
location.
the viaduct is located on the rhaetian railway's metre gauge line from chur to the holiday and recreation resort of arosa (the chur-arosa line), between peist and langwies, at a place known as matten-grundji.

The Rhaetian Railway maintains several entities, including infrastructure, rolling stock, and specific railway lines. Here is a comprehensive list:

1. **Landwasser Viaduct**: This iconic single-track, six-arched curved limestone railway viaduct is maintained by the Rhaetian Railway. It is a signature structure of the World Heritage-listed Albula Railway.

2. **Albula Railway**: A UNESCO World Heritage Site, this railway line is known for its stunning engineering feats and scenic routes through the Swiss Alps. The Rhaetian Railway is responsible for its maintenance.

3. **Bernina Railway**: Another UNESCO World Heritage Site, this line connects St. Moritz in Switzerland to Tirano in Italy. The Rhaetian Railway maintains this line, which is famous for its high-altitude route and breathtaking views.

4. **Pontresina Railway Station**: Located at the junction of the Bernina and Samedan–Pontresina lines, this station is maintained by the Rhaetian Railway. It includes a large station building and several tracks.

5. **Landquart-Davos G 3/4 Locomotives**: The Rhaetian Railway maintains preserved examples of these historic steam locomotives, which are occasionally used for steam train services.

6. **Samedan–Pontresina Railway Line**: This line is part of the Rhaetian Railway network and is maintained by them. It connects the towns of Samedan and Pontresina in the canton of Graubünden.

7. **Arosa Railway**: This line runs from Chur to Arosa and is maintained by the Rhaetian Railway. It is known for its scenic route through the Swiss Alps.

8. **Glacier Express**: While not solely operated by the Rhaetian Railway, they maintain the sections of the route that run through their network. This famous train offers panoramic views of the Swiss Alps.

9. **Engadin Railway**: This line runs through the Engadin valley and is maintained by the Rhaetian Railway. It connects several key locations in the region.

10. **Disentis/Mustér to Scuol-Tarasp Line**: This line is part of the Rhaetian Railway network and is maintained by them, providing important regional connectivity.

These entities highlight the Rhaetian Railway's role in maintaining a diverse and historically significant railway network in Switzerland.

Milos Raonic has won several competitions throughout his tennis career. Here is a comprehensive list of the competitions he has won:

1. **2011 SAP Open**: Raonic won his first ATP Tour title at the SAP Open in San Jose, California, defeating Fernando Verdasco in the final.

2. **2012 Chennai Open**: He claimed the title by defeating Janko Tipsarević in the final, marking his second ATP Tour victory.

3. **2012 SAP Open**: Raonic successfully defended his title at the SAP Open, again defeating Denis Istomin in the final.

4. **2013 SAP Open**: Raonic won the SAP Open for the third consecutive year, defeating Tommy Haas in the final.

5. **2013 Thailand Open**: He secured the title by defeating Tomáš Berdych in the final, adding another ATP Tour title to his collection.

6. **2014 Citi Open**: Raonic won this ATP 500 event in Washington, D.C., by defeating Vasek Pospisil in the final.

7. **2015 St. Petersburg Open**: He claimed the title by defeating João Sousa in the final, marking his first title in Russia.

8. **2016 Brisbane International**: Raonic won this tournament by defeating Roger Federer in the final, showcasing his strong start to the 2016 season.

These victories highlight Raonic's success on the ATP Tour, particularly in hard court tournaments.

Finnes O'Connell is a visual artist known for creating album artwork. Here is a list of artworks produced by Finnes O'Connell:

1. **When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go? (Billie Eilish)**  
   Finnes O'Connell created the cover artwork for Billie Eilish's debut studio album, "When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go?" released in 2019. The artwork features a haunting image of Eilish sitting on a bed in a dark room, which complements the album's themes of dreams and nightmares.

2. **Don't Smile at Me (Billie Eilish)**  
   O'Connell also produced the artwork for Billie Eilish's debut EP, "Don't Smile at Me," released in 2017. The cover features a minimalist design with Eilish standing against a bright yellow background, reflecting the EP's blend of pop and alternative music styles.

3. **Happier Than Ever (Billie Eilish)**  
   The cover artwork for Billie Eilish's second studio album, "Happier Than Ever," released in 2021, was also created by Finnes O'Connell. The artwork shows Eilish in a contemplative pose, with a soft color palette that contrasts with the album's introspective and emotional content.

These artworks highlight Finnes O'Connell's collaboration with Billie Eilish, contributing to the visual identity of her music releases.

These discoveries were crucial in advancing the understanding of polar regions and contributed significantly to the fields of geography and earth sciences.

The Mutton Bird Islands Group in Tasmania consists of several islands. Here is a comprehensive list of islands that are part of this group:

1. **East Pyramids**: These are three steep, rocky islets located close to the south-western coast of Tasmania. They are part of the Southwest National Park and the Tasmanian Wilderness World Heritage Site.

2. **Flat Witch Island**: This island is part of the Mutton Bird Islands Group and is known for its seabird colonies, particularly mutton birds (short-tailed shearwaters).

3. **Big Witch Island**: Another island in the group, it is similarly important for seabird nesting, including mutton birds.

4. **Little Witch Island**: This smaller island is also part of the group and supports seabird populations.

5. **Maatsuyker Island**: While primarily known for its lighthouse, it is part of the broader Mutton Bird Islands Group and is significant for its wildlife, including mutton birds.

6. **De Witt Island**: This island is part of the group and is known for its rugged terrain and wildlife, including seabirds.

7. **Flat Top Island**: This island is included in the Mutton Bird Islands Group and is a habitat for various seabirds.

8. **Bishop and Clerk Islets**: These are part of the group and are known for their seabird colonies.

These islands are significant for their ecological importance, particularly as breeding sites for seabirds like the short-tailed shearwater, commonly known as the mutton bird.

   Although originally a British series, "The IT Crowd" was broadcast on ABC2, bringing its unique brand of tech-related humor to Australian audiences.

10. **Doctor Who**  
    The iconic British science fiction series "Doctor Who" was also part of ABC2's programming, attracting fans of the long-running show.

These shows represent a diverse range of genres and styles, reflecting ABC2's eclectic programming approach before its rebranding and eventual transition to ABC Comedy and ABC TV Plus.
